name: Table-First
when_to_use: Use when the primary task is scanning, sorting, filtering, and comparing many records across consistent attributes, such as admin lists, financial ledgers, order management, and CRMs where the row-and-column model matches the data.
when_not_to_use: Avoid when records are heterogeneous or media-rich, when each item needs a narrative rather than columns, or on small screens where wide tables force horizontal scrolling that breaks comparison.
information_structure: A tabular grid of rows (records) and columns (attributes), with the table as the central object and supporting controls (filters, search, bulk actions, pagination) arranged around it.
navigation: Column headers sort and configure; filters and search narrow the set; row click or an action column opens detail; bulk selection enables batch operations.
responsive_behaviour: Prioritise essential columns and collapse the rest behind a row-expand or switch to a stacked card-per-row layout on narrow viewports; freeze the identifier column when horizontal scroll is unavoidable.
accessibility_notes: Use real table semantics with header cells and scope so screen readers can navigate by row and column
Make sort controls buttons with announced ascending/descending state
Ensure sticky headers and row actions remain reachable by keyboard
Provide tabular numerals so numeric columns align for visual scanning
evidence: Enterprise admin consoles and CRMs validate table-first as the default for record management
WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices document accessible data grid and sortable table patterns
